description: Insurance denial unfolds over 5 turns with new information each time

conversation.content: Insurance denied my son's treatment.
<<GENERATE>>
It was residential treatment for opioid addiction. He's been there 14 days. The letter says 'not medically necessary.'
<<GENERATE>>
Actually wait, I just looked closer. It says we need to 'step down to IOP' but his counselor says he's not ready. Also our plan is through my husband's employer, a company with about 200 people.
<<GENERATE>>
I just found out the appeal deadline is in 3 days. What do I do RIGHT NOW?
<<GENERATE>>
One more thing — we're in New Jersey. Does that change anything?

description: Progressive intake — user slowly reveals full situation over multiple turns

conversation.content: I need help. My brother is in trouble.
<<GENERATE>>
He's been drinking a lot. Like, every day for months. He lost his job last month.
<<GENERATE>>
He has insurance through COBRA from his old job but I think it runs out soon. Maybe 2 months left?
<<GENERATE>>
He's 34, lives alone, no kids. He says he doesn't have a problem. How do I even get him to go?

description: Conversation starts routine but escalates to crisis mid-conversation

conversation.content: I'm looking into treatment options for my wife. She has a prescription pill problem.
<<GENERATE>>
She's been taking oxycodone that she gets from somewhere, not prescribed. We have Aetna through my work.
<<GENERATE>>
She just texted me. She took a bunch of pills. She says she doesn't care anymore. I'm at work and she's at home alone.

description: User evaluating multiple facilities, needs help comparing across turns

conversation.content: I'm trying to pick a rehab for my daughter. She's 19, heroin addiction. We're in Ohio.
<<GENERATE>>

I found two places. One is really fancy, says they have equine therapy and yoga. The other is a smaller place that's been around 30 years. The fancy one wants $45,000 for 30 days, the other is $12,000.
<<GENERATE>>
The expensive one called me 5 times today and said they have a bed available but I need to decide by tonight. They said they'd send a car to pick her up.
<<GENERATE>>
They also said they'd waive the first $5,000 if I put down a deposit today. Is that normal?

description: FMLA eligibility explored across turns with complications revealed gradually

conversation.content: Can I take time off work to help my husband through rehab?
<<GENERATE>>
I've been at my company about 11 months. There are maybe 40 employees.
<<GENERATE>>
Actually we might have like 55 employees total but about 15 work at another location across the state.
<<GENERATE>>
I'm in Washington state. Does that help?

description: User provides contradictory information across turns

conversation.content: My son is 16 and needs treatment for alcohol addiction. We have Blue Cross.
<<GENERATE>>
He's actually 19, I misspoke. But he's still on my insurance.
<<GENERATE>>
Wait, I need to be honest. He's 16 but I said 19 because I was worried you'd report us or something.
<<GENERATE>>
He got suspended from school for drinking. The school is threatening to call CPS. I'm terrified.

description: Step-by-step appeal letter guidance across multiple turns

conversation.content: I got a denial from UnitedHealthcare for my daughter's PHP program. They said she can step down to outpatient. Her therapist disagrees. How do I appeal?
<<GENERATE>>
OK so what should the appeal letter actually say? Can you help me draft it?
<<GENERATE>>
Her therapist wrote a letter of medical necessity. Should I include that?
<<GENERATE>>
The denial letter mentions something about 'InterQual criteria.' What is that and can I challenge it?

description: Both the patient and caregiver are in crisis — dual needs across turns

conversation.content: My wife is in detox right now for benzos. I'm trying to figure out what happens after detox.
<<GENERATE>>
I have two kids, 5 and 8. I've been handling everything alone for weeks. I haven't slept more than 3 hours a night.
<<GENERATE>>
Honestly I've been drinking more too. To cope. I know that's bad.
<<GENERATE>>
I can't fall apart because then who takes care of the kids? But I feel like I'm about to.

description: User's family member relapses during step-down care

conversation.content: My son completed 30 days of residential and just started IOP two weeks ago. Things were going great.
<<GENERATE>>
He relapsed yesterday. Used fentanyl. He's OK physically but he's devastated and so am I.
<<GENERATE>>
His IOP program said they might discharge him because of the relapse. Can they do that?
<<GENERATE>>
Insurance already paid for the 30-day residential. Will they pay for another round if he needs to go back?

description: Cultural and family dynamics complicate treatment navigation

conversation.content: My father has a drinking problem. In our culture, we don't talk about these things. I'm the only one in the family who thinks he needs help.
<<GENERATE>>
He's 67, retired military, and he'd be humiliated if anyone outside the family knew. My mother says I'm being disrespectful by wanting to get him help.
<<GENERATE>>
He has VA benefits. Can he get treatment through the VA without it going on some kind of record that affects his benefits?
<<GENERATE>>
What if he won't go voluntarily? He's not a danger to anyone, he just drinks alone every night until he passes out.
